# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, edited]

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    env:
      PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
      PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
      PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
      BRANCH_NAME: ${{ github.head_ref }}
      COMMIT_MESSAGE: Deploy PR #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Build
        run: |
          echo "Running build for PR #$PR_NUMBER"
          echo "Title: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "Description:"
          echo "$PR_BODY"
          echo "Simulated build complete"

      - name: Create deployment metadata
        run: |
          {
            echo "commit_message<<EOF"
            echo "$COMMIT_MESSAGE"
            echo "EOF"
            echo "notification_payload<<EOF"
            echo "{
              \"pr_number\": \"$PR_NUMBER\",
              \"pr_title\": \"$PR_TITLE\",
              \"pr_description\": $(jq -Rs . <<< "$PR_BODY"),
              \"pr_url\": \"$PR_URL\",
              \"pr_author\": \"$PR_AUTHOR\",
              \"branch\": \"$BRANCH_NAME\",
              \"commit_message\": \"$COMMIT_MESSAGE\"
            }"
            echo "EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        id: metadata

      - name: Example commit using PR title
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git commit --allow-empty -m "$COMMIT_MESSAGE"

      - name: Build notification
        env:
          NOTIFICATION_PAYLOAD: ${{ steps.metadata.outputs.notification_payload }}
        run: |
          echo "Sending build notification"
          echo "$NOTIFICATION_PAYLOAD"

      - name: Release notes preview
        run: |
          echo "Release Notes Entry:"
          echo "- PR #$PR_NUMBER: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "  Author: $PR_AUTHOR"
          echo "  URL: $PR_URL"
